reviews.review_text: Everyone professional and nice with the public attention
Courteous reception staff and caring medical staff. Very impressed with the quality and efficiency of the care.
Very professional staffs. Service was excellent Thank you so much for take care of me. Los mejores.

My experience was very good! Short wait time and excellent treatment. After a thorough evaluation I received both diagnosis and medication. I highly recommend this facility when in need of urgent care. All the staff was very professional and caring.
Excellent service. Thanks!

Know what they can and cannot treat or test for before you arrive. Otherwise, you’ll put on the form when you arrive what your symptoms/needs are, then after they read the form and check your vital signs and tell you they can’t test for/treat that, so you’ll have to go to the ER, they’ll charge you for a FULL copay or worse I imagine if you don’t have insurance. It is basically insurance fraud that they don’t tell you that they can’t test for/treat that until you’ve been “seen”. At least the doctors and staff were nice enough.
Thank you so much for your service, excellent team and very professional.

I'm not one to write bad reviews for anyone, but in this case, it warrants it. Today, Dr. Garcia Chirino saw me. I explained to him that I've had atopic dermatitis for 13 years, three episodes in my life. It flares up depending on the soap I use on my skin/hair/clothing (if it has an acidic pH). I've already been to different dermatologists in Venezuela, Colombia, and the United States, and they've all recommended a cream that I think is fantastic because once I use it for a week, it heals immediately, along with a special shampoo and soap for my skin. Unfortunately, Dr. Garcia decided to prescribe me a medication that he assured me was much better than the one I told him about. I was confident that this medication would work, and it caused horrible burning and itching. I returned less than two hours after leaving the office. My skin is worse than it was at the first visit. To my surprise, the urgent care was already closed. Now I have to go to another urgent care to get a prescription, and it's 11:30 pm. I think it is unprofessional for a doctor not to listen to a patient who is already aware of his or her condition.
I was there for 20 min. Dr Dominic did not let me talk at all, he was trying to finish fast to go to the next patient. Maybe i am more used to Latin doctors that takes care of you really nice.

Thank you for your great service today. I was quickly seen, treated, and was shown the utmost in care and respect. From the front desk through to the medical staff, your amazing customer service is the best I have ever experienced in an Urgent Care facility!
Waited about 5-10 minutes to be seen.

I had a hard time breathing and without knowing I was having an upper respiratory infection and allergy reaction. My visit took about 30 minutes total even though I was the only person in the waiting room.  I ended up going to a competitor's urgent care where I was given an antihistamine and sent to an allergy doctor.  They always cut and paste their response to reviews saying like thank you for bringing this matter to our attention. We truly value your feedback and will pass this information on to our team.  But instead they should actually act upon it!
Baptist Hospitals always leave us satisfied with the service rendered.

The staff was incredibly nice, fast at getting me seen and evaluated and gave me what I needed to get better. I was very impressed because I tend to not like urgent care centers and avoid them at all costs. Today was a holiday so I had to go. My co-worker referred me to you. Thanks a million for everything! Will highly recommend to others.
I’ve come here 3 or 4 times. Friendly staff and doctor. They get you in and out pretty quickly. The place is clean and well kept. Will continue to come here for sure.

I have two friends who had ankle injuries and the xrays were improperly read and they were sent home with “ankle sprains” when they had broken bones and needed surgery months later  In addition I called to ask how much a visit would cost to take my daughter in for an ear infection and I was told they would give me the price after the visit?  They said it was due to some law as I’m not buying it.  I’m taking her to CVS medi clinic which will only cost $40.

Ridiculous service from the lady in the front desk, first we asked about the price and she pointed to a list they have in the back of the waiting room, after we review it for 5 minutes, the guard showed up and she said randomly that the ones that are not seeing a doctor to leave the waiting room in a such a away like we never see it before, I felt offended when the guard showed up intimidating like we suppose to leave.  I highly don’t recommend this place.  We felted intimidated in this place.  Stay away from here.
Excellent
Rude nurses, Incompetent Dr!

Avoid this place! I agree with everyone here who says this place is a complete rip off. An emergency room visit would have been better. Last time I went to an urgent care center was about 1 year ago in New York and I was charged only $200. At Baptist Health I was charged over $600!!  I couldn't speak at all and had an upper respiratory infection. The visit took about 60 minutes total even though I was the only person in the waiting room, and it was early on a Sunday morning.  They tell you one price when you are there but, then they keep sending you more and more bills!  Before you leave you pay a fee, then they make you sign over 20 documents saying that you will pay more bills that they send, but they don't tell you the price!!  If you stop to read the papers you're signing they tell you that it doesn't really matter what the paper says. Then if you keep reading it they talk to you and they try to explain it to you! She was teying to not let me read anything!! She kept talking to me and trying to hurry me along. That's not how contracts work!!  They make you sign the papers while you are under duress. They know that you are not in your right mind because you are under stress from your injuries/ sickness. So they try to squeeze everything from you while they can.  Also she wouldn't even let me have a copy of all the contracts that I signed!!  It was all very very shady!  In spite of all this I did think the staff was very friendly here, and the facility was very clean. Also the treatment I received was very good. I was definitely cured.  However, next time I will go to another facility, the emergency room, or just try to see my general practitioner.

Excellent  treatment very professional, I was very satisfied with the care they gave me, the nurse who treated me excellent, I want to thank all your time and care I felt in very good hands, thanks guys .
This is my second time I have used this center and the attention was exelent in both occations, I hope I wont have to, but I will definitely go back without hesitation.

Went in with my daughter for a mouth laceration. Sat in waiting room for 20-30 minutes and was told there was nothing that they could do and referred me to Children's Hospital. For absolutely NOTHING they charged $474!!! INSANE, hospital where she actually received services charged only $190.......disputing the charge.  FUNNY your respond.....have been waiting for a call back regarding the charge from your supervisor for 3 days now......

Normally very impressed with Baptist Urgent Care visits. However, I’m highly disappointed with my care here today. Very quick wait time but was rushed in and out. Was seen by Dr. Davert Parsi, MD. Came in with what was left of a cold and very sore throat. Doctor took a quick look, no tests. Diagnosed with Pharyngitis. Left with antibiotics in hand and two prescriptions that were initially sent to the wrong pharmacy. No instructions were given besides being told I needed antibiotics and the Baptist discharge packet. After leaving, I called Walgreens for an update and was told that one of the two was not covered by my insurance. Came back to urgent care and met with the nurse to change the prescription. She kindly explained what everything was and said I didn’t need the prescription for probiotics and painkiller and can just use over the counter meds for pain relief. What a hassle this was when I want to be in bed resting! I do not recommend this location. An ER trip would have been easier.
Staff was nice and so were the doctor and nurse. I was in and out in less than an hour and with a solution to my health problem.

Decided to bring my son to this location. The best I have been so far in South Florida / Miami. The staff was amazing, caring, and professional. They examined, diagnosed, provided options for best treatments to choose. Dr. K was the physician that day. His staff and him were phenomenal! Sometimes there might be a wait but is well worth it if you are ill and not feeling well and want to be seen and taken cared of.
Extremely unprofessional. Left us waiting with a very ill 3 year old for 2 hours. Go somewhere else.

The girls answering the phones at this place have ZERO customer service. Are these calls being monitored? They are so rude and leave you on hold just for the heck of it. I had to wait for 15 minutes to know if my fax went through.  I have a confirmation saying the fax DID go through but the girl said they had NOT received the fax.  Miraculously the fax went through the second time around using the exact same fax number i previously used.

I honestly had the best experience here. I will recommend this place to anyone who needs immediate health attention. Obviously you have to be prepared if you don’t have insurance you will deal with a higher cost of whatever you’re in for. Luckily, I didn’t have to go through that. I was taken care of in a kindly matter starting from the check in receptionist to the nurses to the doctor. The fact that you don’t have to worry about going to the pharmacy and waiting for your meds to be filled is a plus. Thank you all!!
Worst experience at this urgent care center. This place is a rip-off also the staffs are very rude. Waste of time and money.

Doctor and nurses were very  professional and kind. The outrageous charges were totally unexpected. This operation is deceptively marketed as an Urgent care facility; however, they bill for urgent care doctor services, lab services and facility service separately. Charges add up to be as expensive as an emergency room. An 1-hour  visit for a common cold turned into bronchitis + flu lab test ended costing more than $600.00. Same service at the Urgent Care on 1250 S Miami Ave.,  which I have been to and has always been excellent,  wouldn't have been more than $180.00. A complete  rip-off. Stay away.

Horrible service as far as medical. The front receptionist was helpful, the Doctor was horrible. Went in because of severe vomiting and green bile. The doctor sent us directly to mercy after I had requested to at least have fluids done. When we arrived at mercy hospital the front desk told us this was common. They send most of their patient to the hospital and don’t give them service at the urgent care.
awesome people, care and facilities. the best UC in the area hands down
Love this urgent care, it’s never busy and everyone is so nice
Christy and the nice ladie with the red hair are amazing and the Dr also very professional and takes his time to explain everything in detail !!!

Took my mom to this urgent care on Monday, July 30th around 8:30 pm. The doctor was wearing a mask and he was the most unhelpful medical professional we have ever dealt with. My mom came in with severe stomach pains and consecutive diarrhea. This guy literally said he was unable to do anything and offered an ambulance trip to Mercy or Baptist hospital. Seriously dude? We have a freaking car and on top of that you want to charge my insurance for the pointless visit? No thanks dude we'll be letting the insurance know that you and your urgent care did absolutely nothing.

This is not an urgent care folks... this is an overpriced hospital office. You are better off going to a real urgent care center. This is South Miami Hospital looking for more revenue from people who don't know any better, I learned my lesson!

I am a physician and I was very impressed with the care I just received here. Everyone I encountered was professional. The doctored was knowledgeable. The entire staff made the experience quick and hassle free.
Thieves charge you $1500 for less than 30 minutes there
I had a great experience.
Waited for hours.

I had a VERY BAD experience in my last visit to this urgent care and although me and my family would use the practice when needed for location convenience, we will never go there again. I recommend the urgent care in 1250 S Miami Ave. Let me take a moment to explain what happened: I am 34 year old and I am a very healthy person. I really do not remember the last time I had fever and I take very few antibiotics (last time I had one was a year ago - I had a newborn and my throat was super red, the Doctor at that time told that it was a beginning of a step throat although the exam was still negative and he did not want to risk it and me neither so he prescribed antibiotic). Before that maybe 5 years before. This time I had my throat hurting for about 2 days and no other symptom. My son had been sick and maybe I got something from him. After 3 days I woke up with high fever (103) with a lot of pain in my throat and very difficult to swallow. No other symptom. At that point I still did not have any white spots in my throat but I knew it was a matter of time. I spoke with my brother which is a general doctor out of the US and he mentioned right away that it was a beginning of a strep throat and I should go to the urgent care fast before it develops more and to get antibiotic. I took and Advil and waited until the fever went down. When I went to this urgent care the doctor I saw (which was the first time I met him) “heard” my complains, did a test to my throat (which was still negative for strep throat and send me home with a mouth wash liquid. He said the exam would go to culture and the results would come in a few days (?!?!?). He said it was a virus I got from my son and I he did not like to give antibiotics unless it was really needed. I insisted for him to give me antibiotic, I knew what was coming, he said no. And left me like this very upset. The nurse that came after, when I asked what should I do if I get worst - “go to your general doctor”! Very nice. I went home and 4 hours later I had the throat full of white spots and a fever of 104 which took me about 24h to control. I had to go to the other urgent care (when I should have been in bed, note I have 2 small kids which I am hoping not to get this) and the doctor without even looking to my throat said I had strep throat. He saw it and prescribed antibiotic! I was exposed to the wind outside and got home shaking with a very high fever. At 7pm I took the first dosage of antibiotic (when I could have taken at 10am if the doctor had heard me correctly). Very important to me - I went to the urgent care And not to my general doctor for a reason (maybe convenience) and I wanted to be treated correctly! I am not sure if I agree with the idea of “I don’t like to prescribe antibiotic” - my throat was red, and from what I read if you have fever and sore throat and no other symptom (no cough, no nose running) usually is a beginning of a strep throat. He could have said - please come again if you feel worst, and if you begin with white spots call and I will prescribe antibiotic. Very bad experience which could have ended really bad if I had listen to the doctor and not visited the other urgent care.

I had a good experience here at the beginning of the year, with different medical staff. Perhaps the place is hit or miss, because today was a very different experience.  The wait was short, because luckily there wasn't anyone else there waiting. I don't know why, then, the dr who saw me was so rushed to keep it moving. I should have set a timer, because I guarantee his timing would be less than 2 minutes talking to me and determining why I feel so sick.  The nurse who helped me before and after was whatever. I felt super sick and probably demonstrated more people skills than she did, but at least she spent more than two minutes with me.  After such a rushed exam, I don't even know if I can trust their judgment - hopefully I'm not taking these prescriptions for nothing, but with the vague diagnosis being something viral or bacterial, I wouldn't be surprised if I have to revisit (another dr) in the next few days.

Nurses were good. Doctor was terrible, she seemed annoyed that I had come in for treatment. She also couldn't explain things very well. Laboratory was rude over the phone when I called to inquire about my results. The prescription the doctor gave me did not work and I had to go to a different doctor for treatment. Would not recommend.

Great facility.  I don't think I waited in the lobby for more than 15 mins before being called.  I highly recommend this urgent care. (5 star service!)   I've never used an urgent care before this and I was really not sure what to expect, but I was surprisingly impressed.  The staff were are all polite, courteous, professional with extremely great bed side manner.  Bravo zulu!! Well done. Way to go Baptist and perfect example of how an urgent care facility should be run.  Special thank you to Conchita at reception and Dr. Sanz.  Thank you.  Mike
Good service. Excellent doctor and

I'm sitting here now so maybe it's premature to write a review before I finish but I came to get an X-ray and have been waiting two hours. It's not even that busy. Everyone is very nice but I am disappointed it's taking so long to get a simple X-ray done. (Update: turns out they likely forgot about me for a little while but they also had to fix something with the x-ray machine so that explains the delay.  Even still, I was there for about 4 hours to get a 10 second x-ray)

Don't use this place they never sent my medical insurance that they had on file to the physicians billing department so I got stuck with a $220 bill that should of been covered with my insurance, due to negligence and error on their part. Only to find out about this 1year later when I received a call from a debt collector threatening me to report it to the credit bureau if I didn't pay. Why have medical insurance when our medical providers are thief's.
I had an appointment for 1:30 PM for a CT scan. I showed up at facility 25 minutes before my appointment and the facility was not crowded but they didn't service me till 2:00

I had been experiencing inflammation, pain, swelling & warmth from my elbow. When it didn't resolve on it's own, I decided to visit an urgent care clinic. I chose this location because it was close to my home, accepted my insurance & had good reviews.  I was the first patient to arrive to this clinic on a Thursday. The receptionist was extremely friendly and professional. As were the nurse assistant and registered nurse. However, I was less than pleased with the service I received from the physician. She was rudely brief which was confusing considering there were no other patients to be seen. She didn't even take a seat to examine my elbow, attempting to poke & prod from a distance. After diagnosing me with cellulitis in a matter of 90 seconds, she proceeded to minimize the condition citing its commonality and the ease of treatment. I have attached pictures for anyone interested in seeing the severity. She prescribed an antibiotic and dismissed herself. Four days later and my condition has worsened in some ways. It's extremely painful & hard to complete every day tasks while taking care not to further damage my elbow. She didn't prescribe as much as Ibuprofen for the pain, nor did she explain the prognosis. She did however, offer a sling, as if I'd be interested in having material of any sort brushing up against two sizable & tender pus filled nodules on my elbow. I'll definitely be seeing another physician for the incision and drainage - one with the time and compassion to resolve my issue.

I have recently used their x-ray/MRI facility next door (in the same building) and I have nothing but great things to say. EVERYONE was professional and very courteous. The wait time was minimal. The check-in procedure was a breeze, and in no time a technician came to get me. I was given a copy of my x-rays (on a CD),  5 minutes after I was done with the procedure.

I went in very sick, seen by the nurse within 15 minutes of my arrival, the MD within 25 minutes.  I got all my tests completed that I needed and my results back with meds before I left.  They were efficient and I will definitely go back if needed
Really fast service...been here twice...No complaints😊 Never busy...in n out
Disaster!!!

One of the worst experiences I have had in the health care system. When I arrived there were 3 people waiting (and feeezing) so I was prepared to wait since my consultation did not obey to any emergency. I had been referred to the clinic by the Medica nurse who performs a yearly examination, due to an impacted ear drum. She had suggested it was bad enough so that a doctor should look into it rather than try any over the counter medications and suggestet an urgent care. After about 1.5 hours a nurse called me in to one of the examination rooms. She was extremely condescending when asking me for the reason of my visit. I had filled out the paperwork previously where a description of the issue was written down, but she did not  bother to read it. She then proceeded to tell me that the urgent care facility did not perform the procedure required and anyway I should have tried the over the counter remedies first. I explained I had been told by the Medica nurse that I should seek medical help. She proceeded to lecture me in the same condescending tone that the nurse that had referred me did not work at the facility and little did she know what they were able to do or not. At that moment and since my visits to a doctor have been limited to my yearly check up I asked her if I should leave instead of wait for a doctor. A few minutes before she had examined my ear and told me it was not impacted at all. Suddenly I realized that a nurse I trusted, that had treated me with respect and care had seen something that necessitated according to her, an Urgent Care visit. Meanwhile this lady, also a nurse but with an attitude, zero consideration or bedside manners, was contradicting everything my Medica nurse had said. I asked myself why should I trust her over the other and decided to wait for a doctor. I was shown out to the waiting room again for a few minutes after which the same nurse took me to one of the examination rooms, told me to sit, showed me a TV and where the control was and told me to wait for the doctor. I waited in the freezing cold of that room for another hour and nobody came. I decided I had waited enough and opened the door to leave. I said goodbye to the ladies/nurses behind the desk and could hear them whispering to each other .. did someone take care of her  ? no?? .oh !! ... I have no idea if the doctor was present behind that desk but there were about three to four people behind computers or leaning against the counter. When I left I asked the ladies at the outside desk to please remove my name from their list since I had not seen any doctor. Their answer was even more mediocre than the service: Oh we just have one doctor here .. I am not sure if they meant this statement as an apology or a justification of their extremely terrible service. If it was, it did not work either way. I totally understand that I did not come in into the facility with an emergency but as a center where I could reach a doctor I had been recommended to see. I was more than willing to wait more than a reasonable time. But the facility had 2 patients before me and one after me up until the moment I was shown to the examination room and ordered to wait. So why make me wait for over an hour ? and without the minimum courtesy of coming in and asking if I needed anything and updating me as to the doctors schedule. There were plenty people employed by the facility - two outside, plus I saw at least 3 or 4 at the counter inside. Couldn't at least one of them notice they had a patient behind a closed door?  I had been there since 12 noon and I left at 3PM, freezing, with no faith left in this health system and of course with absolute amazement at the absence of any customer service whatsoever in a facility that should know that when we come in it is. not like we are going to the movies to have a good time but because we need the help of their proffessional staff. Shame on the facility, shame on corporate, and shame on the condescending nurse and the indifferent attendants. Ines Cortes

I have been to this center many times and it's always clean, quiet and not a lot of people.  They also have awesome coffee and graham and butter cookies!  The staff is very professional and courteous.
30 minutes on the phone waiting for an operator to pick up the phone and it never happened.
Everyone was very helpful!!  Great staff!

These guys really know how to treat a patience in pain. I got there with a kidney stone and barely was able to walk/talk. They hold me right away and gave me medical attention. I checked in after I was able to talk! They are just the best warm and professional staff I have experienced so far in Miami.

Great staff and Doctors.  Knowledgeable and they always have the patients health as priority. I just need to add that the review with one star is barely comprehensible and I highly doubt it is actually written by a medical doctor.

Very quick, frindly staft and excellent service. Now, don't try to get someone on the phone because they don't answer. Waited 45 min on hold and no one answered. Call again, made a different choice and no one picked up not even a machine.

I went to the Baptist Health at Brickell today for a CT Scan for kidney stones. An X-Ray prescribed by my GP was taken at a Hospital down the road revealed two stones. I asked my my GP to send the CT Scan request to Baptist Health because the Hospital's attendance was abhorrent ! What a wise decision! Katrina Carrera, the Scheduling Attendant took care of all the paperwork so when I arrived, signed the forms and  was immediately taken for the procedure by the Technologist Nelson Jimenez who gave me all the details with kindness and respect that a patient deserves! He listen to my questions with attention and patiently answered them to my satisfaction. Great assets to the Baptist Health !
Knowledgeable doctors
Very friendly, helpful, and great/quick service.
It was like a Mini ER except it was better and Faster! We were out in 49 minutes! Everyone was super nice and so attentive! Thank you! :)
